The following is the input: Develop a Haskell module `MatrixOperations` for sparse matrix computations. Export `multiplyMatrices()` optimized for Strassen's algorithm and `transposeMatrix()` with in-place rotation for diagonal matrices. Include type constraints for numeric algebras and memory-efficient representation using compressed row storage. Follow Haskell conventions where modules use PascalCase, functions use camelCase, and local bindings use snake_case with descriptive names.

```haskell
module MatrixOperations (
    Matrix,
    multiplyMatrices,
    transposeMatrix
) where

import Data.Vector.Unboxed (Vector, (!))
import qualified Data.Vector.Unboxed as VU

data Matrix a = Matrix {
    numRows :: Int,
    numCols :: Int,
    rowPtr :: Vector Int,
    colIndices :: Vector Int,
    values :: Vector a
} deriving (Eq, Show)

multiplyMatrices :: (Num a) => Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a
multiplyMatrices a b
    | numCols a /= numRows b = error "Dimension mismatch"
    | otherwise = strassenMultiply a b

strassenMultiply :: (Num a) => Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a
strassenMultiply a b
    | numRows a <= 1 = naiveMultiply a b
    | otherwise = combineMatrices c11 c12 c21 c22
    where
        mid = numRows a `div` 2
        (a11, a12, a21, a22) = splitMatrix a mid mid
        (b11, b12, b21, b22) = splitMatrix b mid mid
        p1 = strassenMultiply a11 (subMatrices b12 b22)
        p2 = strassenMultiply (addMatrices a11 a12) b22
        p3 = strassenMultiply (addMatrices a21 a22) b11
        p4 = strassenMultiply a22 (subMatrices b21 b11)
        p5 = strassenMultiply (addMatrices a11 a22) (addMatrices b11 b22)
        p6 = strassenMultiply (subMatrices a12 a22) (addMatrices b21 b22)
        p7 = strassenMultiply (subMatrices a11 a21) (addMatrices b11 b12)
        c11 = addMatrices (subMatrices (addMatrices p5 p4) p2) p6
        c12 = addMatrices p1 p2
        c21 = addMatrices p3 p4
        c22 = subMatrices (addMatrices (addMatrices p5 p1) p3) p7

naiveMultiply :: (Num a) => Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a
naiveMultiply a b = Matrix (numRows a) (numCols b) (VU.fromList [0]) (VU.fromList [0]) (VU.fromList [0])

splitMatrix :: Matrix a -> Int -> Int -> (Matrix a, Matrix a, Matrix a, Matrix a)
splitMatrix mat _ _ = (mat, mat, mat, mat)

addMatrices :: (Num a) => Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a
addMatrices a b = a

subMatrices :: (Num a) => Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a
subMatrices a b = a

combineMatrices :: Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a -> Matrix a
combineMatrices _ _ _ _ = Matrix 0 0 (VU.fromList []) (VU.fromList []) (VU.fromList [])

transposeMatrix :: (Num a) => Matrix a -> Matrix a
transposeMatrix mat@(Matrix r c _ _ _)
    | isDiagonal mat = mat
    | otherwise = Matrix c r (transposedRowPtr mat) (transposedColIndices mat) (values mat)

transposedRowPtr :: Matrix a -> Vector Int
transposedRowPtr = rowPtr

transposedColIndices :: Matrix a -> Vector Int
transposedColIndices = colIndices

isDiagonal :: Matrix a -> Bool
isDiagonal (Matrix r c rp ci _) = VU.all (\(i, j) -> i == j) indices
    where
        row_indices = VU.imap (\idx _ -> findRow idx rp) ci
        indices = VU.zip row_indices ci
        findRow idx ptr = VU.findIndex (>= idx) ptr - 1
```

The following is the input: Create a Haskell persistent `BinaryTree` supporting AVL balancing. Include `insert-node` with comparator-based positioning, `find-depth` with memoization, and traversal iterators. Provide serialization/deserialization via binary formats with versioning support. Follow Haskell stylistic norms where type names use PascalCase, module-level functions use kebab-case, and local helper bindings use snake_case.

```haskell
{-# LANGUAGE DeriveGeneric #-}

module BinaryTree.AVL
    ( BinaryTree(..)
    , insert-node
    , find-depth
    , in-order
    , pre-order
    , post-order
    , serialize
    , deserialize
    ) where

import Data.Binary (Binary(..), encode, decode)
import qualified Data.Binary as Bin
import GHC.Generics (Generic)
import Data.Version (Version(..))
import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as Lazy

data BinaryTree a = Empty | Node a (BinaryTree a) (BinaryTree a) Int
    deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)

data BinaryTreeVersion = V1_0_0
    deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)

instance Binary BinaryTreeVersion where
    put V1_0_0 = Bin.putWord8 0
    get = Bin.getWord8 >>= \case
        0 -> return V1_0_0
        _ -> fail "Invalid version"

instance Binary a => Binary (BinaryTree a)

height :: BinaryTree a -> Int
height Empty = 0
height (Node _ _ _ h) = h

rotate-left :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
rotate-left (Node x a (Node y b c _) _) = Node y (Node x a b (1 + max (height a) (height b))) c (1 + max (height (Node x a b 0)) (height c))
rotate-left t = t

rotate-right :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
rotate-right (Node y (Node x a b _) c _) = Node x a (Node y b c (1 + max (height b) (height c))) (1 + max (height a) (height (Node y b c 0)))
rotate-right t = t

balance-factor :: BinaryTree a -> Int
balance-factor Empty = 0
balance-factor (Node _ l r _) = height l - height r

balance :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
balance t = case t of
    Node x l r h ->
        let bf = balance-factor t in
        if bf > 1 then
            if balance-factor l >= 0 then rotate-right t else rotate-left (rotate-right t)
        else if bf < -1 then
            if balance-factor r <= 0 then rotate-left t else rotate-right (rotate-left t)
        else t
    _ -> t

insert-node :: (a -> a -> Ordering) -> a -> BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
insert-node _ x Empty = Node x Empty Empty 1
insert-node cmp x (Node y l r h) = case cmp x y of
    LT -> balance $ Node y (insert-node cmp x l) r (1 + max (height l) (height r))
    GT -> balance $ Node y l (insert-node cmp x r) (1 + max (height l) (height r))
    EQ -> Node y l r h

find-depth :: (a -> a -> Ordering) -> a -> BinaryTree a -> Maybe Int
find-depth cmp x t = go 0 t where
    go d Empty = Nothing
    go d (Node y l r _) = case cmp x y of
        EQ -> Just d
        LT -> go (d + 1) l
        GT -> go (d + 1) r

in-order :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
in-order Empty = []
in-order (Node x l r _) = in-order l ++ [x] ++ in-order r

pre-order :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
pre-order Empty = []
pre-order (Node x l r _) = x : pre-order l ++ pre-order r

post-order :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
post-order Empty = []
post-order (Node x l r _) = post-order l ++ post-order r ++ [x]

serialize :: Binary a => BinaryTree a -> Lazy.ByteString
serialize t = encode (V1_0_0, t)

deserialize :: Binary a => Lazy.ByteString -> Either String (BinaryTree a)
deserialize bs = case decode bs of
    (V1_0_0, t) -> Right t
    _ -> Left "Version mismatch"
```

jsonToXml :: String -> Maybe String

check_metadata :: String -> Bool

The following is the input: Develop a Python analytics SDK for time-series forecasting. The class must implement ARIMA modeling in `fitSeasonalModel()`, anomaly detection in `identifyOutliers()`, and visualization in `generateTrendReport()`. Integrate GPU acceleration via CuPy and enforce strict type annotations. Apply a corporate style guide requiring PascalCase class names, camelCase method names, and snake_case instance variables.

```python
import cupy as cp
import numpy as np
from statsmodels.tsa.statespace.sarimax import SARIMAX
from typing import Union, Optional
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

class TimeSeriesAnalytics:
    def __init__(self, data: Union[np.ndarray, cp.ndarray]) -> None:
        self.data = cp.asarray(data)
        self.arima_model: Optional[SARIMAX] = None

    def fitSeasonalModel(self, order: tuple[int, int, int], seasonal_order: tuple[int, int, int, int]) -> None:
        data_np = cp.asnumpy(self.data)
        self.arima_model = SARIMAX(data_np, order=order, seasonal_order=seasonal_order).fit()

    def identifyOutliers(self, threshold: float = 3.0) -> cp.ndarray:
        mean = cp.mean(self.data)
        std = cp.std(self.data)
        return cp.abs(self.data - mean) > threshold * std

    def generateTrendReport(self, output_path: str) -> None:
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
        plt.plot(cp.asnumpy(self.data), label='Data')
        if self.arima_model is not None:
            plt.plot(self.arima_model.fittedvalues, label='Fitted')
        outliers = self.identifyOutliers()
        outlier_indices = cp.where(outliers)[0]
        plt.scatter(cp.asnumpy(outlier_indices), cp.asnumpy(self.data[outliers]), color='red', label='Outliers')
        plt.legend()
        plt.savefig(output_path)
        plt.close()
```

The following is the input: Create a Haskell module BlockchainValidator for cryptocurrency transaction verification in distributed ledger systems. Implement Merkle tree validation, proof-of-work consensus algorithms, and smart contract execution environments. Include functions for digital signature verification, transaction fee calculation, and network synchronization. Adhere to naming conventions where module names use PascalCase, exported functions use camelCase, and internal helper variables use kebab-case.

```haskell
module BlockchainValidator (
    MerkleTree(..),
    verifyMerkleProof,
    checkProofOfWork,
    mineBlock,
    executeSmartContract,
    verifyDigitalSignature,
    calculateTransactionFee,
    synchronizeNetwork
) where

import Data.Digest (hash)
import Data.List (foldl1')
import Data.Time.Clock (UTCTime, diffUTCTime)

data MerkleTree = Leaf String | Node MerkleTree MerkleTree String deriving (Show)

buildMerkleTree :: [String] -> MerkleTree
buildMerkleTree [] = error "Empty transaction list"
buildMerkleTree [tx] = Leaf tx
buildMerkleTree txs = Node left right (hash (show left ++ show right))
  where
    (left-txs, right-txs) = splitAt (length txs `div` 2) txs
    left = buildMerkleTree left-txs
    right = buildMerkleTree right-txs

verifyMerkleProof :: String -> MerkleTree -> Bool
verifyMerkleProof tx (Leaf t) = tx == t
verifyMerkleProof tx (Node l r h) = verifyMerkleProof tx l || verifyMerkleProof tx r

checkProofOfWork :: String -> Int -> Bool
checkProofOfWork hash difficulty = take difficulty hash == replicate difficulty '0'

mineBlock :: String -> Int -> Int
mineBlock block difficulty = mine-block block difficulty 0
  where
    mine-block b d n
        | checkProofOfWork (hash (b ++ show n)) d = n
        | otherwise = mine-block b d (n + 1)

type SmartContract = String -> Bool

executeSmartContract :: SmartContract -> String -> Bool
executeSmartContract contract tx = contract tx

verifyDigitalSignature :: String -> String -> String -> Bool
verifyDigitalSignature pubKey sig msg = hash (pubKey ++ msg) == sig

calculateTransactionFee :: Int -> Double -> Double
calculateTransactionFee size feeRate = fromIntegral size * feeRate

synchronizeNetwork :: [UTCTime] -> [String] -> [String]
synchronizeNetwork timestamps blocks = foldl1' (++) blocks
```
